observation: Harder evals reveal two genuine gaps the skill addresses: (1) named vs default import for MultiGraph subclasses — the model defaults to 'import Foo from graphology' which resolves to the base Graph class and breaks parallel edges; (2) raw forceAtlas2() vs forceAtlas2.assign() — without the skill the model defaults to the .assign() convenience method and misses the raw API. The skill explicitly covers both patterns and both pass with_skill on iteration 2.

notable_failures: uses-multidirectedgraph: Default import 'import MultiDirectedGraph from graphology' imports the base Graph class (multi:false); parallel nyc→london edges would throw at runtime.

configurations.qualitative_delta: Uses addDirectedEdge instead of idiomatic addEdge on DirectedGraph. Event names and payload shapes are correct.

notable_failures: forceatlas2-raw-call: Used forceAtlas2.assign() instead of raw forceAtlas2(). These have different return values and different application patterns.
